text:
BOTTOM LINE — Iran crisis deepens, mega-cap tech craters. Large-cap tech sold off as oil surged 5.93% on Iran escalation fears, rotating money into defense, freight, and value sectors while growth and discretionary names were dumped.

INDICES — VIX 18.7 (+5.12%). IWM -0.58%, DIA -1.00%, SPY -1.23%, QQQ -1.90%, SOXX -0.77%. Small > large by 0.65pp. DIA outperformed SPY by 0.23pp. Oil +5.93%, gold -2.00%.

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio 1.2x, QQQ 1.1x — both elevated. QQQ price down 1.90% with above-average volume confirms distribution in tech. XLC and XLY saw heaviest sector volume at 2.0x and 1.7x respectively, both down sharply, indicating panic selling in communication services and consumer discretionary.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLI Industrials +1.73%, XLV Healthcare +1.26%, XLU Utilities +0.57%, XLE Energy +0.30%, XLRE Real Estate -0.13%, XLF Financials -0.39%, XLK Technology -1.01%, XLB Materials -1.04%, XLP Staples -1.39%, XLC Comms -3.50%, XLY Discretionary -4.61%. Top Industries: USO +5.93%, ITA +3.08%. Bottom: MAGS -4.63%, SILJ -3.80%. Money is rotating out of discretionary and comms into industrials, defense, and energy as the Iran crisis drives a classic defensive-commodity rotation. Rotation state: 'defensive risk-off'.

POSITIONING — SPY put-call ratio 1.19 (elevated protection demand), QQQ 0.91 (moderate hedging). VIX at 18.7 is above its 20-day MA of 16.8, signaling elevated fear. MOVE index at 80.1 is above its 20-day MA of 70.2, indicating rising bond vol stress.

CROSS-ASSET — Oil +5.93% vs airlines? Not in data, but gold -2.00% despite risk-off is notable — likely liquidation to cover margin or rebalance into oil. Dollar +0.39% adds pressure on metals.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed 59.4 (Neutral). Top movers: TMO +8.71% (Medtech 7), LMT +10.54% (Defense 7), TSLA -14.52% (Mag 7). Best sector XLI +1.73%, worst XLY -4.61%. Vol spikes: UNP +4.02% (accumulation, vol 3.3x + price up), TMO +8.71% (accumulation), TSLA -14.52% (distribution, vol 3.0x + price down). Watch: LMT tomorrow — defense momentum play on Iran crisis; XLI — rotation leader into industrials with elevated volume; TMO — medtech breakout with massive volume.
